accounts.admin module¶

class accounts.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Bases: UserAdmin

Custom admin panel for the CustomUser model with role displayed.

fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), (None, {'fields': ('role', 'reader_subscriptions')}))¶
list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff', 'is_active')¶
list_filter = ('role', 'is_staff', 'is_active')¶

Ensure email is unique.

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

accounts.models module¶

Custom user model for the News Application.

This model extends Django’s AbstractUser to support role-based access control and subscription features.

Roles supported: - Reader - Journalist - Editor

Readers can subscribe to publishers and journalists to receive updates and newsletters.

class accounts.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model with role-based access control.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

ROLE_CHOICES = (('reader', 'Reader'), ('journalist', 'Journalist'), ('editor', 'Editor'))¶
approved_articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

approved_newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

auth_token¶

Accessor to the related object on the reverse side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

date_joined¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

email¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

first_name¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
get_role_display(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.CharField: role>)¶
groups¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

username¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

accounts.signals module¶

Django signals for the accounts application.

This module handles automatic setup of user groups and permissions when the database is migrated, ensuring proper role-based access control for all users.

accounts.signals.create_groups_and_permissions(sender, **kwargs)¶

Create user groups and assign permissions after migrations.

Sets up Reader, Editor, and Journalist groups with appropriate permissions and assigns each user to their corresponding group based on their role.

accounts.tests module¶

class accounts.tests.CustomUserModelTest(methodName='runTest')¶

Bases: TestCase

classmethod setUpTestData()¶

Load initial data for the TestCase.

test_group_permissions()¶

Check that groups have the correct permissions

test_user_group_assignment()¶

Check that users are assigned to the correct groups

test_user_roles()¶

Check that each user has the correct role and staff status

accounts.urls module¶

accounts.views module¶

Views for user authentication and account management.

This module provides user registration and login views with role-based redirection to user dashboards.

class accounts.views.CustomLoginView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: LoginView

Role-based login view.

authentication_form¶

alias of CustomLoginForm

form_invalid(form)¶

If the form is invalid, render the invalid form.

form_valid(form)¶

Security check complete. Log the user in.

template_name = 'accounts/login.html'¶
accounts.views.redirect_user_by_role(user)¶

Redirect users to their dashboard based on role.

accounts.views.register(request)¶

Role-based registration view.
